title: Trump Team Tries New Way to Add Taxes on Imports After Court Said No
summary: The Trump administration is starting a new process to try to put taxes on imports from other countries. This comes after the Supreme Court said no to their old taxes. They want to protect American jobs by making sure foreign companies don't have an unfair advantage. The investigation will look at countries like China, Mexico, and Japan to see if they have too many goods coming into the U.S. or if they help their companies too much. The goal is to replace the old taxes that were stopped by the court. This could affect trade deals and the economy, and it's happening during elections and other world events.

Stop all trade with other countries
questions.correct_answer: Start a new process to add taxes on imports

options: During elections and other world events

background_read: Tariffs are taxes that countries put on goods from other places to make them more expensive. The Supreme Court is like a referee that decides if laws are fair. In the U.S., the president and his team make trade rules to help American workers. Sometimes, they investigate other countries to see if they are playing fair in trade. This article talks about how the Trump administration is trying a new way to add tariffs after the old ones were stopped by the Supreme Court.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Trump administration and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer
WHAT: Starting a new investigation to add tariffs on imports from other countries
WHERE: In the U.S., looking at countries like China, Mexico, and Japan
WHY: To protect American jobs and replace old tariffs that the Supreme Court said no to

perspectives.description: The administration believes new tariffs are needed to protect American jobs and ensure fair trade.
